meta.title: BACOLOD CITY
meta.subtitle: Negros Occidental, Western Visayas
meta.description: Bacolod City, the City of Smiles, is the capital of Negros Occidental and the economic hub of the Visayas. Famous for its Masskara Festival, heritage mansions, and the sweetest chicken inasal in the Philippines, Bacolod is a city that celebrates life with infectious warmth and joy.

attractions.name: The Ruins
attractions.description: The most iconic landmark of Bacolod — a grand Italianate mansion ruins built by sugar baron Don Mariano Ledesma Lacson, now lit magnificently at night.

attractions.name: Negros Museum
attractions.description: An excellent museum showcasing the natural history, cultural heritage, and sugar industry legacy of Negros Occidental.
attractions.things_to_do: Museum tour, cultural learning, art viewing

attractions.name: Capitol Park & Lagoon
attractions.description: A beautiful public park surrounding the Negros Occidental Capitol building, featuring a lagoon, gardens, and jogging paths.

attractions.name: Bacolod City Plaza
attractions.description: The social heart of Bacolod, where locals gather for events, the Masskara Festival, and everyday urban life.

attractions.name: San Sebastian Cathedral
attractions.description: The main cathedral of Bacolod Diocese, a beautiful historic church that has witnessed centuries of the city's religious life.

attractions.name: Sugar Industry Museum
attractions.description: A museum dedicated to the sugar industry that shaped Negros Occidental's history, economy, and culture over 150 years.

attractions.name: Silay Heritage City
attractions.description: A day trip to the nearby Silay City, the 'Paris of Negros', with well-preserved ancestral houses and a charming heritage atmosphere.
attractions.things_to_do: Heritage walk, ancestral house tours, dining

attractions.name: Balay Negrense Museum
attractions.description: A beautifully restored 19th century sugar baron's house in Silay City now converted into a fascinating heritage museum.
attractions.things_to_do: Museum tour, heritage photography, cultural learning

attractions.name: Campuestohan Highland Resort
attractions.description: A highland resort in the mountains above Bacolod offering cool weather, ziplines, swimming pools, and scenic views.

activities.name: Masskara Festival Experience
activities.description: Experience Bacolod's most famous festival — a week of street dancing, mask-wearing, and pure joy every October.
activities.things_to_do: Dancing, mask wearing, street parades, concerts

activities.name: Chicken Inasal Food Tour
activities.description: A guided tour of Bacolod's famous inasal restaurants and street stalls, tasting the original charcoal-grilled chicken that inspired a national chain.

activities.name: Sugar Plantation Tour
activities.description: Visit a working sugarcane plantation and mill in Negros Occidental to witness the harvest and milling process that built the city.
activities.things_to_do: Plantation tour, sugarcane tasting, cultural learning

activities.name: Masskara Mask Making
activities.description: Learn to decorate and paint your own Masskara papier-mâché mask from local artisans in a fun hands-on workshop.

activities.name: The Ruins Night Tour
activities.description: Experience The Ruins after dark when the grand mansion is illuminated in golden light, creating an enchanting and romantic atmosphere.
activities.things_to_do: Photography, heritage tour, dinner at the ruins

activities.name: Beach Day in Sipalay
activities.description: A day trip to the white sand beaches of Sipalay in southern Negros Occidental for swimming, snorkeling, and island relaxation.

activities.name: Bacolod Golf & Country Club
activities.description: Play a round of golf at one of the best golf courses in the Visayas, set amidst lush greenery with mountain views.

activities.name: Ancestral House Tour
activities.description: A guided tour of Silay City's preserved ancestral houses, getting a glimpse inside sugar baron mansions from the 19th century.

activities.name: Mt. Kanlaon Day Hike
activities.description: Trek to the foothills of the active Mt. Kanlaon volcano, a National Park offering breathtaking views and diverse wildlife.

food.name: Chicken Inasal
food.description: Bacolod's most famous dish — charcoal-grilled chicken marinated in local spices and annatto oil, served with garlic rice and spiced vinegar.

food.name: Manokan Country
food.description: A strip of competing inasal restaurants in the heart of Bacolod — the birthplace of Chicken Inasal and still the best place to taste it.

food.name: Piaya & Napoleones
food.description: Two of Bacolod's most iconic pasalubong sweets — piaya (muscovado-filled flatbread) and napoleones (custard-filled pastry layers).

food.name: Kansi
food.description: A rich sour beef soup unique to Bacolod — slow-cooked beef with bone marrow in a tangy batuan broth served with steamed rice.

food.name: Dining at The Ruins
food.description: An unforgettable dining experience at the restaurant within The Ruins heritage site, serving Negrense cuisine with a romantic backdrop.

food.name: Bacolod Fresh Lumpia
food.description: Fresh spring rolls stuffed with local vegetables, shrimps, and pork, wrapped in a soft crepe and topped with sweet garlic sauce.

food.name: Bacolod Cakes & Sweets
food.description: Bacolod has a thriving cake culture with famous bakeries producing unique ensaimada, sans rival cakes, and chiffon cakes.

food.name: Bob Marlin Restaurant
food.description: A beloved Bacolod institution known for its grilled seafood, live music, and laid-back beach resort atmosphere.

food.name: Muscovado Sugar Products
food.description: Bacolod's rich sugar heritage is tasted in muscovado sugar products — from raw cane sugar to artisanal sweets and pastries.

events.name: Masskara Festival
events.description: The City of Smiles' grandest celebration every October — a dazzling week of smiling mask street dances, concerts, food fairs, and fireworks.
events.things_to_do: Street dancing, concerts, food fair, fireworks

events.name: Panaad sa Negros Festival
events.description: A week-long festival showcasing the unique culture, festivals, and products of each municipality in Negros Occidental.
events.things_to_do: Cultural shows, trade fair, sports events

events.name: Halad Festival
events.description: A religious festival honoring the patron saint of Bacolod through processions, street events, and thanksgiving celebrations.

events.name: Negros Trade Fair
events.description: An annual trade fair highlighting Negros Occidental's agricultural products, handicrafts, and local food specialties.

events.name: Bacolod Christmas Parade
events.description: One of the grandest Christmas parades in the Visayas, featuring elaborate floats, marching bands, and a festive atmosphere.

events.name: Sugar Industry Festival
events.description: A celebration of the 150-year sugar industry heritage of Negros Occidental with exhibits, tours, and cultural programs.

events.name: Bacolod City Marathon
events.description: A major running event in the Visayas drawing thousands of runners through Bacolod's streets every year.

events.name: Negros Art Gallery Exhibit
events.description: A rotating gallery exhibit at the Negros Museum showcasing works by prominent Negrense artists and national artists.

events.name: Bacolod Food Festival
events.description: A celebration of Bacolod's rich food culture featuring the city's best restaurants, street food stalls, and cooking competitions.

accommodations.name: L'Fisher Hotel
accommodations.description: Bacolod's most prestigious hotel, offering luxury rooms, a rooftop pool, and fine dining in the heart of the city.
accommodations.things_to_do: Pool, fine dining, city center access

accommodations.name: Seda Capitol Central
accommodations.description: A modern upscale hotel adjacent to Bacolod's Capitol Lagoon offering stylish rooms and contemporary amenities.

accommodations.name: Sugarland Hotel
accommodations.description: A classic Bacolod hotel named after the sugarcane heritage of Negros, offering comfortable rooms and a large pool area.

accommodations.name: The Henry Hotel Bacolod
accommodations.description: A hip boutique hotel with art-inspired design, a rooftop bar, and a curated blend of contemporary and heritage aesthetics.

accommodations.name: Terraces Hotel
accommodations.description: A well-appointed hotel with multiple pools, lush gardens, and a prime location near Bacolod's main commercial district.

accommodations.name: Metro Centre Hotel
accommodations.description: A centrally located hotel connected to a major shopping mall, perfect for convenience and easy access to the city.

accommodations.name: Almont Inland Resort
accommodations.description: A resort-style hotel with multiple pools, event facilities, and a relaxing garden environment a short drive from the city center.

accommodations.name: Golden Sky Hotel
accommodations.description: A mid-range hotel offering clean and comfortable accommodations at reasonable prices in the heart of Bacolod.

accommodations.name: Planta Centro Bacolod
accommodations.description: A modern budget-friendly hotel with stylish rooms in the center of Bacolod City, perfect for value-conscious travelers.
